items.title: Andrew Pinsent - Can Philosophy Clarify Science vs. Religion?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Get member exclusives like early access to new content with a free Closer To Truth account: https://closertotruth.com/

Science and theology have a tumultuous history of conflict and strife on the one hand, and attempts to seek consilience and consonance on the other. Can philosophy help discern what may be true from what is clearly false? Which principles or methods of philosophy can adjudicate science vs theology?

Closer To Truth is now on BlueSky! Follow us for updates, new videos, musings, and more: https://shorturl.at/ypXl2

Fr. Andrew Pinsent is professor of theology and philosophy at the Athenaeum of Ohio in Cincinnati, Ohio, and a Catholic priest of the Diocese of Arundel and Brighton in England.

items.title: Eric Schwitzgebel - Metaphysics vs. Materialism?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Closer To Truth is now on BlueSky! Follow us for insights, updates, and more: https://shorturl.at/twLQn

Metaphysics asks the most profound questions, then uses sophisticated philosophical analysis to seek the deepest truths. What happens when metaphysics trains its analytical guns on ‘materialism’, the claim that only the physical is real? What are the metaphysical arguments for and against materialism?

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Eric Schwitzgebel is an American professor of philosophy at the University of California, Riverside. His main interests include connections between empirical psychology, philosophy of mind, and the nature of belief, as well as Classical Chinese philosophy.

items.title: Richard Swinburne on God’s Nature & the Coherence of Theism | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Contribute what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

What is God like? Is God truly omniscient and omnipotent? What is God's relationship to time? Philosopher Richard Swinburne explores these questions and more in this virtual interview.

Richard Granville Swinburne FBA is an English philosopher. He is an Emeritus Professor of Philosophy at the University of Oxford. Over the last 50 years, Swinburne has been a proponent of philosophical arguments for the existence of God. His philosophical contributions are primarily in the philosophy of religion and philosophy of science. He aroused much discussion with his early work in the philosophy of religion, a trilogy of books consisting of "The Coherence of Theism", "The Existence of God", and "Faith and Reason". He has been influential in reviving substance dualism as an option in philosophy of mind.

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e</article>

items.title: Quayshawn Spencer - Sex, Sexuality, & Gender
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Like Closer To Truth on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

Sexual questions abound. What is “normal” sexual behavior vs. multiple variations? How to appreciate LGBTQ+ issues? What can be learned from sex in animal models that is relevant for human sexuality? A philosophical perspective can provide clarity.

items.title: Bernardo Kastrup - Altered States, Psychedelics, and Consciousness Theories?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Register for a free Closer To Truth membership to receive special exclusives like early access to new episodes and more: https://closertotruth.com/.

Altered states of consciousness, such as induced by meditative practices or ingesting hallucinogens, radically alter one’s consciousness. But do these altered states provide veritable evidence about the nature of consciousness?

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for new videos, announcements, and more: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

Bernardo Kastrup is a Brazilian-born Dutch philosopher and computer scientist best known for his work in the field of consciousness studies, particularly his development of analytic idealism, a form of metaphysical idealism grounded in the analytic philosophical tradition.

items.title: Christopher Southgate - How does God Relate to the World?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

If God is the Creator, what is God’s on-going relationship with the creation? Does God sustain the world by an active process, such that if God stopped doing so, the world would disappear? Or does the world now have a kind of self-existence independent of God? Moreover, does God ever make special interventions into the world, otherwise known as ‘miracles’?

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Christopher Southgate is Research Fellow in Theology at the University of Exeter, UK.

items.title: Annaka Harris on Questions About Consciousness | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Robert Lawrence Kuhn poses rapid-fire questions on consciousness to Annaka Harris, including inquiries like: Is consciousness fundamental? What is required for consciousness? What things are conscious? Can Ai ever be conscious?

Annaka Harris is the New York Times bestselling author of "Conscious: A Brief Guide to the Fundamental Mystery of the Mind" and writer and producer of the new audio documentary series, "Lights On: How Understanding Consciousness Helps Us Understand the Universe".

<article>In the second part of our extensive interview with Annaka Harris, we trace the arc of her new documentary's journey by going through each chapter and exploring the experts she interviewed for the project—including Sean Carroll, Brian Greene, Janna Levin, Philip Goff, and many more.

items.title: Etzel Cardeña - Consciousness and Life After Death?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Get member exclusives like early access to new content with a free Closer To Truth account: https://closertotruth.com/

Life after death is explored in the context of diverse theories of consciousness, from strict materialism/physicalism to those of ancient wisdom traditions. We explore the view that the haunting and deeply personal question of life after death relates to theories of consciousness.

Closer To Truth is now on BlueSky! Follow us for updates, new videos, musings, and more: https://bsky.app/profile/closertotruth.bsky.social

Etzel Cardeña holds the Thorsen Chair in psychology including parapsychology and hypnosis at Lund University, Sweden. He was elected Fellow of the American Psychological Association (APA) and the Association for Psychological Science (APS), among other organizations. His research, theoretical, historical, and pedagogical contributions have received 25 awards from various academic and professional organizations.

items.title: Richard Dawkins - Philosophy of Extended Evolutionary Synthesis
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Closer To Truth is now on BlueSky! Follow us for insights, updates, and more: https://shorturl.at/twLQn

What is the extended evolutionary synthesis (EES) and why better? Includes multilevel selection, transgenerational epigenetic inheritance, niche construction, evolvability, and ideas from evolutionary developmental biology. Why is EES controversial?

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Richard Dawkins is a British evolutionary biologist, zoologist, science communicator, and author. He is an emeritus fellow of New College, Oxford, and was Professor for Public Understanding of Science in the University of Oxford

items.title: Denis Alexander - Fallacies in Proving God Exists?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

The more we want God to exist, the more we must question so-called ‘proofs’ of God’s existence. God likely appreciates skeptics, those who genuinely seek and genuinely doubt. Skeptics provide real service: they help clear up or clean out poor arguments for God. Is it possible all the ‘God arguments’ are bad?

Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Dr. Denis Alexander has spent 40 years in the biomedical research community. He is an Emeritus Fellow of St. Edmund’s College, Cambridge and an Emeritus Director of the Faraday Institute for Science and Religion, Cambridge which he co-founded with Bob White in 2006.

items.title: Dean Zimmerman - Are There Multiple Universes?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merch purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

What is the extent of God’s creation? If there are multiple universes, did God create each of them? If an infinite number of universes, did God create all of them? Theists may be conflicted: the centrality of human beings in this one universe, and, for Christians, the incarnation on Earth, versus the magnification of God with multiple universes.

Closer To Truth is now on BlueSky! Follow us for updates, perspectives, and more: https://shorturl.at/2eJCn

Dean W. Zimmerman is an American professor of philosophy at Rutgers University specializing in metaphysics and philosophy of religion.

items.title: Robert Saucy - A New Heaven & A New Earth?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Contribute what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

‘A new heaven and a new earth’ is the grand vision of the Bible. The Judeo-Christian scriptures tell of a future time when all things shall be made new. What could this mean? Does ‘all’ include the entire cosmos? Multiple universes, if there are such? Could such an ancient vision ever make sense? Believers say, “It’s God’s mystery.” They should say more.

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for daily updates and exciting announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

Robert Lloyd Saucy was an American biblical scholar and professor of systematic theology.

items.title: Liad Mudrik - Theories of Consciousness
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Sign up for a free Closer To Truth membership to receive special exclusives like early access to new episodes and more: https://closertotruth.com/.

Explore diverse theories of consciousness including materialism, dualism, panpsychism, idealism and diverse ways of thinking about consciousness from the perspectives of neuroscience, philosophy, wisdom traditions. Compare and contrast diverse theories of consciousness as they are arrayed on a “Landscape of Consciousness”.

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for new videos, announcements, and more: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

Professor Liad Mudrik is a researcher at the school of psychological sciences and Sagol school of neuroscience at Tel Aviv University. Her research focuses on conscious experience, its neural mechanisms and functions.
